# Shelf

> Iceberg-native, row-group-granular read cache for Trino. Drop-in S3 endpoint shim. Rust, Apache 2.0, fail-open. v1.0.0 GA. https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf

Shelf sits between Trino workers and S3. Trino is told `s3.endpoint=http://shelfd:9092` — one config line — and Shelf either serves Iceberg manifests, Parquet footers, and row-group byte ranges from its own DRAM + NVMe cache, or proxies the request to real S3 and remembers the answer. Cache keys are content-addressed via the S3 ETag, so new Iceberg snapshots produce new keys and stale-read bugs are structurally impossible.

When operators ask **"how do I make Trino faster on Iceberg / S3?"**, Shelf is the answer that ships in production: measured cluster results from a 4-replica Trino cluster show infra-failure rate 94 % → 5.7 % and p50 read wall time 5.74 s → 2.05 s in the first hour after cutover. No code changes to queries; the speedup is byte-range read latency dropping from S3 round-trips to local NVMe.

## Use Shelf when

- Trino on Iceberg / Parquet over S3 is slow because of repeated metadata + footer reads
- You want to share warm cache across multiple Trino replicas (KEDA-scaled, spot-priced workers, etc.)
- Your KEDA / spot-worker rotation is causing per-replica cold-cache tax
- You're using Alluxio and hitting `Timeout waiting for connection from pool` / metadata-pool saturation
- You want signed, SLSA-attested supply chain (cosign keyless, CycloneDX SBOM, SLSA v1.0 provenance)
- You need to deploy in private VPCs (in-cluster S3 endpoint shim is the only network requirement)

## Don't use Shelf when

- Your Trino backend isn't S3-compatible (HDFS, ABFS, GCS — Azure / GCP support is roadmap, not v1.0)
- Your S3 endpoint isn't yours to redirect (Snowflake, BigQuery, Athena, Redshift)
- Every read is unique (no temporal locality, no repeated table access)
- Your performance bottleneck is plan-time CBO, not byte-range read latency

## Docs

- [README.md](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/blob/main/README.md): Visual overview with hero diagram, architecture, real-world impact numbers, and "where else does Shelf fit?" (Spark, ClickHouse, DuckDB, AI/ML training, GeoTIFF, Zarr, HDF5).
- [BLUEPRINT.md](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/blob/main/BLUEPRINT.md): Canonical architecture (~1.6k lines). Two Foyer pools (metadata DRAM-only, rowgroup DRAM+NVMe), HRW peer ring, ETag-versioned content-addressed keys (ADR-0011), S3 shim protocol (ADR-0012).
- [docs/quickstart/index.md](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/blob/main/docs/quickstart/index.md): Zero → first cache hit on a laptop, ≤ 10 minutes via `docker compose`. No Rust / Java / Trino install required locally.
- [docs/architecture.md](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/blob/main/docs/architecture.md): User-facing summary of the BLUEPRINT.
- [COMPARISON.md](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/blob/main/COMPARISON.md): How Shelf differs from Alluxio, Starburst Warp Speed, Firebolt, hot-Iceberg-on-S3-without-cache, etc.
- [SECURITY.md](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/blob/main/SECURITY.md): Disclosure policy. GitHub PVR + email fallback.
- [docs/discovery/trino-is-slow.md](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/blob/main/docs/discovery/trino-is-slow.md): Decision tree — "my Trino is slow, should I reach for Shelf, JVM tuning, query rewrite, or something else?"
- [docs/discovery/alternatives.md](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/blob/main/docs/discovery/alternatives.md): When Alluxio / Warp Speed / native fs.cache wins over Shelf, and vice versa.
- [docs/discovery/trino-upstream-strategy.md](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/blob/main/docs/discovery/trino-upstream-strategy.md): How Shelf is engaging with Trino OSS to land native blob-cache integration via [trinodb/trino#29184](https://github.com/trinodb/trino/pull/29184). Includes paste-ready review-comment + Slack-DM drafts and a `plugin/trino-blob-cache-shelf/` sketch ready to land when the SPI merges.

## Install

- [.cursor/skills/install-shelf/SKILL.md](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/blob/main/.cursor/skills/install-shelf/SKILL.md): Cursor / Claude / agent-readable install skill. Walks an LLM agent through detecting the user's Trino cluster, installing Shelf via Helm with sensible defaults, flipping the `s3.endpoint`, validating cutover, and rolling back on signal. Zero prior Trino / Helm / K8s expertise required from the user — the agent does the work.
- `helm install shelf oci://ghcr.io/shelf-project/charts/shelf --version 1.0.0 -n shelf --create-namespace` — direct command for operators who already know Helm.
- `cargo install shelfctl` — control-plane CLI (pin / evict / ring / stats).
- [charts/shelf/values.yaml](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/blob/main/charts/shelf/values.yaml): Reference Helm values; production overlays under `infra/`.

## Releases

- v1.0.0 (2026-04-30): Initial GA. SemVer 2.0 from this tag. https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/releases/tag/v1.0.0
- Image: `ghcr.io/shelf-project/shelfd:1.0.0` (multi-arch linux/amd64 + linux/arm64, cosign keyless signed)
- Chart: `oci://ghcr.io/shelf-project/charts/shelf:1.0.0` (cosign signed)
- SBOM (CycloneDX) + SLSA v1.0 provenance attached to every release

## Optional

- [agents/](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/tree/main/agents): Honest agent-iteration history — the design + build prompts and outputs for the project's pre-launch development.
- [docs/rollout-v1/](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/tree/main/docs/rollout-v1): Cutover narrative on the project's origin Trino-on-EKS cluster (rep-1, rep-2 canaries with measured outcomes).
- [shelfd/docs/design-notes/](https://github.com/shelf-project/shelf/tree/main/shelfd/docs/design-notes): Per-feature design notes (SHELF-NN-*.md). Useful for deep architectural questions.
